array.name: Array
array.description: The most fundamental linear data structure
objectives: Understand memory layout of arrays
Master index-based access
Analyze time and space complexity

linked_list.name: Linked List
objectives: Understand the basic structure of a linked list
Master node insertion and deletion
Analyze time complexity

doubly_linked_list.name: Doubly Linked List
doubly_linked_list.description: Advanced linked structure supporting bidirectional traversal
objectives: Understand the advantages of doubly linked lists
Master forward and backward pointer management
Apply to LRU Cache

stack.name: Stack
objectives: Understand the LIFO principle
Master push/pop operations
Apply to function call stacks

queue.name: Queue
objectives: Understand the FIFO principle
Master enqueue/dequeue operations
Apply to breadth-first search

binarytree.name: Binary Tree
binarytree.description: A fundamental tree structure where each node has at most two children
objectives: Understand tree structure and node relationships
Master pre-order, in-order, and post-order traversal
Understand the use of recursion in trees

bst.name: Binary Search Tree
bst.description: A binary tree with sorted properties: left subtree is smaller than root, right subtree is larger
objectives: Understand the sorted properties of BST
Master search, insertion, and deletion logic
Analyze the impact of balance on performance

heap.name: Heap
heap.description: A complete binary tree structure supporting fast retrieval of the maximum or minimum value
objectives: Understand the difference between Min Heap and Max Heap
Master the heapify operation
Apply to priority queues and sorting

graph.name: Graph
graph.description: A non-linear data structure composed of vertices and edges
objectives: Understand the basic components and terminology of graphs
Compare adjacency matrix and adjacency list
Distinguish between directed and undirected graphs

trie.name: Trie
trie.description: An N-ary tree structure designed for string prefix queries
objectives: Understand the node structure of a Trie
Master insertion and lookup operations
Apply to autocomplete and spell checking

bubble_sort.name: Bubble Sort
bubble_sort.description: The most basic sorting algorithm, sorting by repeatedly swapping adjacent elements
objectives: Understand the basic concepts of sorting algorithms
Master the logic of nested loops
Analyze time complexity O(n²)

selection_sort.name: Selection Sort
selection_sort.description: Repeatedly selects the minimum from the unsorted portion and places it at the end of the sorted portion
objectives: Understand the core logic of selection sort
Compare bubble sort and selection sort
Analyze time complexity O(n²)

insertion_sort.name: Insertion Sort
insertion_sort.description: Inserts each element into its correct position in the sorted portion
objectives: Understand the implementation of insertion sort
Master applicable scenarios (small-scale data)
Analyze best and worst cases

merge_sort.name: Merge Sort
merge_sort.description: A stable sorting algorithm using the divide-and-conquer approach
objectives: Understand the core idea of divide and conquer
Master recursive implementation techniques
Analyze time complexity O(n log n)

quick_sort.name: Quick Sort
quick_sort.description: A highly efficient sorting algorithm using divide and conquer
objectives: Understand the partitioning logic of quick sort
Master pivot selection strategies
Analyze average time complexity O(n log n)

linear_search.name: Linear Search
linear_search.description: Sequentially checks each element until the target value is found
objectives: Understand the basic concept of linear search
Compare with binary search
Analyze time complexity O(n)

binary_search.name: Binary Search
binary_search.description: Quickly finds the target value in a sorted array
objectives: Understand the principle of binary search
Master boundary condition handling
Analyze time complexity O(log n)

factorial.name: Recursion Basics: Factorial
factorial.description: Understand the recursive call and return flow through factorial
objectives: Understand the basic structure of recursion (base case / recursive case)
Master the operation of the function call stack
Visualize the recursive expansion and return process

fibonacci_recursive.name: Recursion Tree: Fibonacci Sequence
fibonacci_recursive.description: Understand recursion trees and overlapping subproblems through the Fibonacci sequence
objectives: Understand how a recursion tree unfolds
Identify overlapping subproblems
Compare the performance of recursion vs. iteration

merge_sort_recursive.name: Divide & Conquer: Merge Sort
merge_sort_recursive.description: Revisit merge sort's divide-and-conquer structure from a recursive perspective
objectives: Understand the three steps of divide and conquer (divide, solve, merge)
Visualize the recursion tree and merge process
Analyze the time complexity of divide and conquer

hanoi.name: Tower of Hanoi
hanoi.description: Classic recursion problem: move all disks from peg A to peg C
objectives: Understand the unfolding logic of multi-level recursion
Master recursive formula derivation
Visualize each move step

backtracking_intro.name: Introduction to Backtracking
backtracking_intro.description: Systematically explore all possible solutions and backtrack when a path is infeasible
objectives: Understand the decision tree model of backtracking
Master pruning strategies
Apply to permutation and combination problems

quick_sort_recursive.name: Divide & Conquer: Quick Sort
quick_sort_recursive.description: Understand quick sort's pivot partitioning strategy from a recursive perspective
objectives: Understand how pivot selection affects recursion tree depth
Master best, average, and worst case analysis
Visualize the partition process

n_queens.name: N-Queens Problem
n_queens.description: Classic backtracking application: place N non-attacking queens on an N×N chessboard
objectives: Apply backtracking to constraint satisfaction problems
Master pruning logic for chessboard state
Visualize the search and backtrack process

prefix_sum.name: Prefix Sum
prefix_sum.description: A preprocessing technique for fast range sum queries
objectives: Understand how to build a prefix sum array
Master O(1) range queries
Apply to frequent query scenarios

sliding_window.name: Sliding Window
sliding_window.description: Maintain a sliding interval over an array or string using two pointers to avoid redundant computation, optimizing time complexity to O(N).
objectives: Master the sliding logic of two-pointer interval maintenance
Accurately identify when to expand or shrink the window
Apply to solving problems involving specific conditions on contiguous intervals (e.g., maximum sum of fixed length, shortest subarray meeting a target)

two_pointers.name: Two Pointers
two_pointers.description: Use two pointers to collaboratively traverse an array or string, reducing time complexity
objectives: Understand same-direction and opposite-direction two pointers
Master the two-sum problem in a sorted array
Apply to palindrome detection in strings

bit_mask.name: Bit Manipulation
bit_mask.description: Use bitwise operations to achieve efficient state compression and computation
objectives: Understand basic bitwise operators
Master common bit tricks (parity check, swap, powers of two)
Apply to bitmask DP

bfs.name: Breadth-First Search
bfs.description: A graph traversal algorithm implemented using a queue
objectives: Understand the traversal order of BFS
Master the use of queues
Apply to shortest path problems

dfs.name: Depth-First Search
dfs.description: A graph traversal algorithm implemented using a stack or recursion
objectives: Understand the traversal order of DFS
Master recursive implementation techniques
Apply to path-finding problems

topological_sort.name: Topological Sort
topological_sort.description: Linearly order the nodes of a directed acyclic graph (DAG)
objectives: Understand the use cases of topological sort
Master Kahn's Algorithm (BFS-based)
Apply to course scheduling and dependency resolution

union_find.name: Union-Find
union_find.description: A data structure for efficiently managing disjoint sets
objectives: Understand Find and Union operations
Master path compression optimization
Apply to connected component detection

dijkstra.name: Dijkstra's Shortest Path
dijkstra.description: A classic algorithm for finding single-source shortest paths in a weighted graph
objectives: Understand the greedy strategy and the use of a priority queue
Master the edge relaxation operation
Analyze why it cannot handle negative-weight edges

astar.name: A* Search
astar.description: A shortest path search algorithm incorporating a heuristic function
objectives: Understand the estimation logic of f(x) = g(x) + h(x)
Master the design principles of heuristic functions
Compare A* with Dijkstra

dp_fibonacci.name: DP Intro: Fibonacci Sequence
dp_fibonacci.description: A complete evolution from recursion to memoization to bottom-up DP
objectives: Understand memoized search (Top-Down)
Master building a Bottom-Up DP table
Compare the performance of recursion, memo, and DP

knapsack.name: 0/1 Knapsack Problem
knapsack.description: A classic DP problem: select items of maximum value within a weight limit
objectives: Understand DP state definition and transition equations
Master the process of filling a 2D DP table
Optimize to a 1D rolling array

lcs.name: Longest Common Subsequence
lcs.description: Find the longest common subsequence of two sequences
objectives: Understand the state transition logic of LCS
Master the table-filling animation and subsequence backtracking
Apply to diff tools and genomic alignment

data_structures.name: Data Structures
data_structures.description: Learn the working principles and applications of various data structures

sorting.name: Sorting Algorithms
sorting.description: Master the implementation and performance analysis of various sorting algorithms

searching.name: Searching Algorithms
searching.description: Explore efficient search strategies and techniques

recursive.name: Recursion & Divide and Conquer
recursive.description: Master recursive thinking and divide-and-conquer strategies to build a foundation for DP

technique.name: Algorithm Techniques
technique.description: Learn general problem-solving patterns and optimization techniques

graph.name: Graph Algorithms
graph.description: Deeply understand the core algorithms of graph theory

dp.name: Dynamic Programming
dp.description: Solve overlapping subproblems through state transitions and master optimization thinking
